import json

# Get all 2015 article_ids from the metadata file
file_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:0']
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    metadata_2015 = json.load(f)

# Process in batches to avoid query size limits
def chunk_list(lst, chunk_size):
    for i in range(0, len(lst), chunk_size):
        yield lst[i:i + chunk_size]

# Get article_ids from metadata
article_ids = [int(item['article_id']) for item in metadata_2015]
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_articles': len(article_ids),
    'first_10': article_ids[:10],
    'last_10': article_ids[-10:]
}))

# Load existing results

    metadata_2015 = json.load(f)

remaining = len(batches)

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({'total_batches': len(batches), 'total_articles': len(article_ids)}))

    'Business': ['stock', 'market', 'IPO', 'company', 'business', 'economy', 'trade', 'deficit', 'profit', 'revenue', 'shares', 'investment', 'bank', 'financial'],
    'Science/Technology': ['technology', 'tech', 'science', 'research', 'study', 'discovery', 'innovation', 'digital', 'software', 'hardware', 'internet', 'computer']
}

# Load metadata

with open(meta_file, 'r') as f:
    metas = json.load(f)

# Sample more articles from different regions

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'sample_size': len(sample_ids),
    'sample_ids': sample_ids[:10]
}))

args.code:
import json
from collections import Counter
import re

# Load metadata
meta_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:0']
with open(meta_file, 'r') as f:
    metas = json.load(f)

# Region lookup
region_lookup = {int(m['article_id']): m['region'] for m in metas}

# Keywords for World category (international relations, conflicts, diplomacy, global issues)
world_keywords = [
    'war', 'conflict', 'peace', 'treaty', 'diplomatic', 'ambassador', 'embassy', 'foreign', 'relations',
    'border', 'territory', 'province', 'military', 'forces', 'attack', 'bomb', 'violence', 'protest',
    'united nations', 'u.n.', 'security council', 'international', 'global', 'world', 'nuclear',
    'africa', 'europe', 'asia', 'south america', 'north america', 'middle east', 'israel', 'palestine',
    'afghanistan', 'iraq', 'iran', 'syria', 'lebanon', 'pakistan', 'india', 'china', 'russia',
    'climate change', 'global warming', 'pandemic', 'epidemic', 'humanitarian', 'aid', 'refugee'
]

# Keywords to exclude (for other categories)
sports_keywords = ['football', 'soccer', 'basketball', 'baseball', 'tennis', 'golf', 'olympic', 'championship', 'tournament', 'coach', 'player', 'team', 'season', 'league', 'game', 'win', 'lose', 'score']
business_keywords = ['stock', 'market', 'IPO', 'company', 'business', 'economy', 'trade', 'deficit', 'profit', 'revenue', 'shares', 'investment', 'bank', 'financial', 'earnings', 'sales']
scitech_keywords = ['technology', 'tech', 'science', 'research', 'study', 'discovery', 'innovation', 'digital', 'software', 'hardware', 'internet', 'computer', 'NASA', 'space', 'DNA', 'genetic', 'medical']

sample_articles = {
    13: "Google IPO Auction Off to Rocky Start",
    18: "US trade deficit swells in June",
    26: "Google auction begins on Friday",
    51: "Delightful Dell",
    52: "Chrysler's Bling King",
    116: "NASA's Genesis Spacecraft Adjusts Course",
    141: "Indictments Using DNA on Rise Nationally",
    2041: "Hollywood awaits Termite from Texas and Iraqi fly",
    2168: "Crude rises on Iraq troubles",
    2214: "US Men Squander Lead, But Hold Off Thorpe to Win"
}

def categorize_article(title, description):
    """Categorize article based on title and description"""
    text = f"{title} {description}".lower()
    
    # Count matches for each category
    world_matches = sum(1 for kw in world_keywords if kw in text)
    sports_matches = sum(1 for kw in sports_keywords if kw in text)
    business_matches = sum(1 for kw in business_keywords if kw in text)
    scitech_matches = sum(1 for kw in scitech_keywords if kw in text)
    
    # Return category with most matches
    categories = {
        'World': world_matches,
        'Sports': sports_matches,
        'Business': business_matches,
        'Science/Technology': scitech_matches
    }
    
    # Get category with max matches
    max_matches = max(categories.values())
    if max_matches == 0:
        return 'Other'
    
    # Return category with most matches
    max_categories = [cat for cat, count in categories.items() if count == max_matches]
    return max_categories[0]  # Return first if tie

# Test categorization
test_cases = [
    ("Google IPO Auction", "Google IPO description"),
    ("War breaks out in region", "International conflict"),
    ("NASA launches new probe", "Space exploration"),
    ("Team wins championship", "Sports victory")
]

test_results = [categorize_article(title, desc) for title, desc in test_cases]

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_2015_articles': len(metas),
    'region_counts': {region: len([m for m in metas if m['region'] == region]) for region in ['Europe', 'South America', 'Africa', 'Asia', 'North America']},
    'test_categorization': test_results
}))

val_args.code:
import json
from collections import Counter
import re

# Load metadata
meta_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:0']
with open(meta_file, 'r') as f:
    metas = json.load(f)

# Region lookup
region_lookup = {int(m['article_id']): m['region'] for m in metas}

# Keywords for World category (international relations, conflicts, diplomacy, global issues)
world_keywords = [
    'war', 'conflict', 'peace', 'treaty', 'diplomatic', 'ambassador', 'embassy', 'foreign', 'relations',
    'border', 'territory', 'province', 'military', 'forces', 'attack', 'bomb', 'violence', 'protest',
    'united nations', 'u.n.', 'security council', 'international', 'global', 'world', 'nuclear',
    'africa', 'europe', 'asia', 'south america', 'north america', 'middle east', 'israel', 'palestine',
    'afghanistan', 'iraq', 'iran', 'syria', 'lebanon', 'pakistan', 'india', 'china', 'russia',
    'climate change', 'global warming', 'pandemic', 'epidemic', 'humanitarian', 'aid', 'refugee'
]

# Keywords to exclude (for other categories)
sports_keywords = ['football', 'soccer', 'basketball', 'baseball', 'tennis', 'golf', 'olympic', 'championship', 'tournament', 'coach', 'player', 'team', 'season', 'league', 'game', 'win', 'lose', 'score']
business_keywords = ['stock', 'market', 'IPO', 'company', 'business', 'economy', 'trade', 'deficit', 'profit', 'revenue', 'shares', 'investment', 'bank', 'financial', 'earnings', 'sales']
scitech_keywords = ['technology', 'tech', 'science', 'research', 'study', 'discovery', 'innovation', 'digital', 'software', 'hardware', 'internet', 'computer', 'NASA', 'space', 'DNA', 'genetic', 'medical']

sample_articles = {
    13: "Google IPO Auction Off to Rocky Start",
    18: "US trade deficit swells in June",
    26: "Google auction begins on Friday",
    51: "Delightful Dell",
    52: "Chrysler's Bling King",
    116: "NASA's Genesis Spacecraft Adjusts Course",
    141: "Indictments Using DNA on Rise Nationally",
    2041: "Hollywood awaits Termite from Texas and Iraqi fly",
    2168: "Crude rises on Iraq troubles",
    2214: "US Men Squander Lead, But Hold Off Thorpe to Win"
}

def categorize_article(title, description):
    """Categorize article based on title and description"""
    text = f"{title} {description}".lower()
    
    # Count matches for each category
    world_matches = sum(1 for kw in world_keywords if kw in text)
    sports_matches = sum(1 for kw in sports_keywords if kw in text)
    business_matches = sum(1 for kw in business_keywords if kw in text)
    scitech_matches = sum(1 for kw in scitech_keywords if kw in text)
    
    # Return category with most matches
    categories = {
        'World': world_matches,
        'Sports': sports_matches,
        'Business': business_matches,
        'Science/Technology': scitech_matches
    }
    
    # Get category with max matches
    max_matches = max(categories.values())
    if max_matches == 0:
        return 'Other'
    
    # Return category with most matches
    max_categories = [cat for cat, count in categories.items() if count == max_matches]
    return max_categories[0]  # Return first if tie

# Test categorization
test_cases = [
    ("Google IPO Auction", "Google IPO description"),
    ("War breaks out in region", "International conflict"),
    ("NASA launches new probe", "Space exploration"),
    ("Team wins championship", "Sports victory")
]

test_results = [categorize_article(title, desc) for title, desc in test_cases]

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_2015_articles': len(metas),
    'region_counts': {region: len([m for m in metas if m['region'] == region]) for region in ['Europe', 'South America', 'Africa', 'Asia', 'North America']},
    'test_categorization': test_results
}))

args.code:
import json
from collections import Counter

# Load metadata
meta_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:0']
with open(meta_file, 'r') as f:
    metas = json.load(f)

# Test categorization on sample data
world_keywords = ['war', 'conflict', 'peace', 'treaty', 'diplomatic', 'ambassador', 'embassy', 'foreign', 'relations', 'border', 'territory', 'province', 'military', 'forces', 'attack', 'violence', 'protest', 'united nations', 'u.n.', 'security council', 'international', 'global', 'world', 'nuclear', 'africa', 'europe', 'asia', 'south america', 'north america', 'middle east', 'israel', 'palestine', 'afghanistan', 'iraq', 'iran', 'syria', 'lebanon', 'pakistan', 'india', 'china', 'russia', 'climate change', 'global warming', 'pandemic', 'epidemic', 'humanitarian', 'aid', 'refugee']

sports_keywords = ['football', 'soccer', 'basketball', 'baseball', 'tennis', 'golf', 'olympic', 'championship', 'tournament', 'coach', 'player', 'team', 'season', 'league', 'game', 'win', 'lose', 'score']

business_keywords = ['stock', 'market', 'IPO', 'company', 'business', 'economy', 'trade', 'deficit', 'profit', 'revenue', 'shares', 'investment', 'bank', 'financial', 'earnings', 'sales']

scitech_keywords = ['technology', 'tech', 'science', 'research', 'study', 'discovery', 'innovation', 'digital', 'software', 'hardware', 'internet', 'computer', 'NASA', 'space', 'DNA', 'genetic', 'medical']

def simple_categorize(title_descr):
    text = title_descr.lower()
    
    categories = {
        'World': sum(1 for kw in world_keywords if kw in text),
        'Sports': sum(1 for kw in sports_keywords if kw in text),
        'Business': sum(1 for kw in business_keywords if kw in text),
        'Science/Technology': sum(1 for kw in scitech_keywords if kw in text)
    }
    
    max_val = max(categories.values())
    if max_val == 0:
        return 'Other'
    
    # Return first category with max value
    for cat, val in categories.items():
        if val == max_val:
            return cat

# Test on known examples
test_results = {
    'Google IPO': simple_categorize('Google IPO description'),
    'War article': simple_categorize('War breaks out in region international conflict'),
    'NASA article': simple_categorize('NASA launches new probe space exploration'),
    'Sports article': simple_categorize('Team wins championship game')
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(test_results))

val_args.code:
import json
from collections import Counter

# Load metadata
meta_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:0']
with open(meta_file, 'r') as f:
    metas = json.load(f)

# Test categorization on sample data
world_keywords = ['war', 'conflict', 'peace', 'treaty', 'diplomatic', 'ambassador', 'embassy', 'foreign', 'relations', 'border', 'territory', 'province', 'military', 'forces', 'attack', 'violence', 'protest', 'united nations', 'u.n.', 'security council', 'international', 'global', 'world', 'nuclear', 'africa', 'europe', 'asia', 'south america', 'north america', 'middle east', 'israel', 'palestine', 'afghanistan', 'iraq', 'iran', 'syria', 'lebanon', 'pakistan', 'india', 'china', 'russia', 'climate change', 'global warming', 'pandemic', 'epidemic', 'humanitarian', 'aid', 'refugee']

sports_keywords = ['football', 'soccer', 'basketball', 'baseball', 'tennis', 'golf', 'olympic', 'championship', 'tournament', 'coach', 'player', 'team', 'season', 'league', 'game', 'win', 'lose', 'score']

business_keywords = ['stock', 'market', 'IPO', 'company', 'business', 'economy', 'trade', 'deficit', 'profit', 'revenue', 'shares', 'investment', 'bank', 'financial', 'earnings', 'sales']

scitech_keywords = ['technology', 'tech', 'science', 'research', 'study', 'discovery', 'innovation', 'digital', 'software', 'hardware', 'internet', 'computer', 'NASA', 'space', 'DNA', 'genetic', 'medical']

def simple_categorize(title_descr):
    text = title_descr.lower()
    
    categories = {
        'World': sum(1 for kw in world_keywords if kw in text),
        'Sports': sum(1 for kw in sports_keywords if kw in text),
        'Business': sum(1 for kw in business_keywords if kw in text),
        'Science/Technology': sum(1 for kw in scitech_keywords if kw in text)
    }
    
    max_val = max(categories.values())
    if max_val == 0:
        return 'Other'
    
    # Return first category with max value
    for cat, val in categories.items():
        if val == max_val:
            return cat

# Test on known examples
test_results = {
    'Google IPO': simple_categorize('Google IPO description'),
    'War article': simple_categorize('War breaks out in region international conflict'),
    'NASA article': simple_categorize('NASA launches new probe space exploration'),
    'Sports article': simple_categorize('Team wins championship game')
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(test_results))

args.code:
import json
from collections import Counter

# Load metadata for 2015
meta_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:0']
with open(meta_file, 'r') as f:
    metas = json.load(f)

# Create lookup tables
region_lookup = {int(m['article_id']): m['region'] for m in metas}

# Process articles in smaller batches (50 at a time)
batch_size = 50
all_articles = [int(m['article_id']) for m in metas]
batches = [all_articles[i:i+batch_size] for i in range(0, len(all_articles), batch_size)]

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_articles': len(all_articles),
    'total_batches': len(batches),
    'last_article_id': all_articles[-1]
}))

args.code:
import json
from collections import Counter
import re

# Load 2015 metadata
meta_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:0']
with open(meta_path, 'r') as f:
    metas = json.load(f)

# Categorization keywords
world_keywords = ['war', 'conflict', 'peace', 'treaty', 'diplomatic', 'ambassador', 'embassy', 'foreign', 'relations', 'border', 'territory', 'province', 'military', 'forces', 'attack', 'violence', 'protest', 'united nations', 'u.n.', 'security council', 'international', 'global', 'world', 'nuclear', 'africa', 'europe', 'asia', 'south america', 'north america', 'middle east', 'israel', 'palestine', 'afghanistan', 'iraq', 'iran', 'syria', 'lebanon', 'pakistan', 'india', 'china', 'russia', 'climate change', 'global warming', 'pandemic', 'epidemic', 'humanitarian', 'aid', 'refugee']
sports_keywords = ['football', 'soccer', 'basketball', 'baseball', 'tennis', 'golf', 'olympic', 'championship', 'tournament', 'coach', 'player', 'team', 'season', 'league', 'game', 'win', 'lose', 'score', 'athlete']
business_keywords = ['stock', 'market', 'IPO', 'company', 'business', 'economy', 'trade', 'deficit', 'profit', 'revenue', 'shares', 'investment', 'bank', 'financial', 'earnings', 'sales', 'fund', 'index']
scitech_keywords = ['technology', 'tech', 'science', 'research', 'study', 'discovery', 'innovation', 'digital', 'software', 'hardware', 'internet', 'computer', 'NASA', 'space', 'DNA', 'genetic', 'medical', 'physics', 'chemistry']

def categorize_article(text):
    """Categorize article text"""
    if not text:
        return 'Other'
    
    text_lower = text.lower()
    
    # Count keyword matches for each category
    world_count = sum(1 for kw in world_keywords if kw in text_lower)
    sports_count = sum(1 for kw in sports_keywords if kw in text_lower)
    business_count = sum(1 for kw in business_keywords if kw in text_lower)
    scitech_count = sum(1 for kw in scitech_keywords if kw in text_lower)
    
    # Determine category
    categories = {
        'World': world_count,
        'Sports': sports_count,
        'Business': business_count,
        'Science/Technology': scitech_count
    }
    
    max_count = max(categories.values())
    if max_count == 0:
        return 'Other'
    
    # Return first category with highest count (consistent ordering)
    for category in ['World', 'Sports', 'Business', 'Science/Technology']:
        if categories[category] == max_count:
            return category
    
    return 'Other'

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({'ready_to_process': True, 'total_articles': len(metas)}))

val_args.code:
import json
from collections import Counter

# Load metadata for 2015
meta_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:0']
with open(meta_file, 'r') as f:
    metas = json.load(f)

article_ids_2015 = [int(m['article_id']) for m in metas]
region_by_id = {int(m['article_id']): m['region'] for m in metas}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_2015_articles': len(article_ids_2015),
    'regions_present': list(set(m['region'] for m in metas)),
    'sample_regions': {str(k): v for k, v in list(region_by_id.items())[:5]}
}))

args.code:
import json

# Load all articles from the query
articles_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:46']
with open(articles_file, 'r') as f:
    all_articles = json.load(f)

# Load 2015 metadata
metadata_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:0']
with open(metadata_file, 'r') as f:
    metas = json.load(f)

# Create lookup for 2015 articles
article_ids_2015 = {int(m['article_id']) for m in metas}
region_by_id = {int(m['article_id']): m['region'] for m in metas}

# Filter articles to only those from 2015
articles_2015 = [a for a in all_articles if int(a['article_id']) in article_ids_2015]

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_all_articles': len(all_articles),
    'total_2015_articles': len(articles_2015),
    'sample': articles_2015[:3]
}))

val_args.code:
import json

# Load all articles from the query
articles_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:46']
with open(articles_file, 'r') as f:
    all_articles = json.load(f)

# Load 2015 metadata
metadata_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:0']
with open(metadata_file, 'r') as f:
    metas = json.load(f)

# Create lookup for 2015 articles
article_ids_2015 = {int(m['article_id']) for m in metas}
region_by_id = {int(m['article_id']): m['region'] for m in metas}

# Filter articles to only those from 2015
articles_2015 = [a for a in all_articles if int(a['article_id']) in article_ids_2015]

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_all_articles': len(all_articles),
    'total_2015_articles': len(articles_2015),
    'sample': articles_2015[:3]
}))

args.code:
import json
from collections import Counter

# Load metadata for 2015
metadata_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:0']
with open(metadata_file, 'r') as f:
    metas = json.load(f)

# Prepare categorization
world_keywords = ['war', 'conflict', 'peace', 'treaty', 'diplomatic', 'ambassador', 'embassy', 'foreign', 'relations', 'border', 'territory', 'province', 'military', 'forces', 'attack', 'violence', 'protest', 'united nations', 'u.n.', 'security council', 'international', 'global', 'world', 'nuclear', 'africa', 'europe', 'asia', 'south america', 'north america', 'middle east', 'israel', 'palestine', 'afghanistan', 'iraq', 'iran', 'syria', 'lebanon', 'pakistan', 'india', 'china', 'russia', 'climate change', 'global warming', 'pandemic', 'epidemic', 'humanitarian', 'aid', 'refugee', 'UN']
sports_keywords = ['football', 'soccer', 'basketball', 'baseball', 'tennis', 'golf', 'olympic', 'championship', 'tournament', 'coach', 'player', 'team', 'season', 'league', 'game', 'win', 'lose', 'score', 'athlete', 'medal']
business_keywords = ['stock', 'market', 'IPO', 'company', 'business', 'economy', 'trade', 'deficit', 'profit', 'revenue', 'shares', 'investment', 'bank', 'financial', 'earnings', 'sales', 'fund', 'index', 'quarter', 'growth', 'economic']
scitech_keywords = ['technology', 'tech', 'science', 'research', 'study', 'discovery', 'innovation', 'digital', 'software', 'hardware', 'internet', 'computer', 'NASA', 'space', 'DNA', 'genetic', 'medical', 'physics', 'chemistry', 'engineering', 'satellite']

def categorize(text):
    if not text:
        return 'Other'
    text_lower = text.lower()
    
    scores = {
        'World': sum(1 for kw in world_keywords if kw in text_lower),
        'Sports': sum(1 for kw in sports_keywords if kw in text_lower),
        'Business': sum(1 for kw in business_keywords if kw in text_lower),
        'Science/Technology': sum(1 for kw in scitech_keywords if kw in text_lower)
    }
    
    max_score = max(scores.values())
    if max_score == 0:
        return 'Other'
    
    # Return category with highest score (consistent ordering)
    for cat in ['World', 'Sports', 'Business', 'Science/Technology']:
        if scores[cat] == max_score:
            return cat
    return 'Other'

# Prepare to process batches
article_ids = [int(m['article_id']) for m in metas]
region_lookup = {int(m['article_id']): m['region'] for m in metas}
batch_size = 100
batches = [article_ids[i:i+batch_size] for i in range(0, len(article_ids), batch_size)]

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_articles': len(article_ids),
    'num_batches': len(batches),
    'test_categorize': categorize('War breaks out in Middle East UN Security Council')
}))

val_args.code:
import json
from collections import Counter

# Load metadata for 2015
metadata_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:0']
with open(metadata_file, 'r') as f:
    metas = json.load(f)

# Prepare categorization
world_keywords = ['war', 'conflict', 'peace', 'treaty', 'diplomatic', 'ambassador', 'embassy', 'foreign', 'relations', 'border', 'territory', 'province', 'military', 'forces', 'attack', 'violence', 'protest', 'united nations', 'u.n.', 'security council', 'international', 'global', 'world', 'nuclear', 'africa', 'europe', 'asia', 'south america', 'north america', 'middle east', 'israel', 'palestine', 'afghanistan', 'iraq', 'iran', 'syria', 'lebanon', 'pakistan', 'india', 'china', 'russia', 'climate change', 'global warming', 'pandemic', 'epidemic', 'humanitarian', 'aid', 'refugee', 'UN']
sports_keywords = ['football', 'soccer', 'basketball', 'baseball', 'tennis', 'golf', 'olympic', 'championship', 'tournament', 'coach', 'player', 'team', 'season', 'league', 'game', 'win', 'lose', 'score', 'athlete', 'medal']
business_keywords = ['stock', 'market', 'IPO', 'company', 'business', 'economy', 'trade', 'deficit', 'profit', 'revenue', 'shares', 'investment', 'bank', 'financial', 'earnings', 'sales', 'fund', 'index', 'quarter', 'growth', 'economic']
scitech_keywords = ['technology', 'tech', 'science', 'research', 'study', 'discovery', 'innovation', 'digital', 'software', 'hardware', 'internet', 'computer', 'NASA', 'space', 'DNA', 'genetic', 'medical', 'physics', 'chemistry', 'engineering', 'satellite']

def categorize(text):
    if not text:
        return 'Other'
    text_lower = text.lower()
    
    scores = {
        'World': sum(1 for kw in world_keywords if kw in text_lower),
        'Sports': sum(1 for kw in sports_keywords if kw in text_lower),
        'Business': sum(1 for kw in business_keywords if kw in text_lower),
        'Science/Technology': sum(1 for kw in scitech_keywords if kw in text_lower)
    }
    
    max_score = max(scores.values())
    if max_score == 0:
        return 'Other'
    
    # Return category with highest score (consistent ordering)
    for cat in ['World', 'Sports', 'Business', 'Science/Technology']:
        if scores[cat] == max_score:
            return cat
    return 'Other'

# Prepare to process batches
article_ids = [int(m['article_id']) for m in metas]
region_lookup = {int(m['article_id']): m['region'] for m in metas}
batch_size = 100
batches = [article_ids[i:i+batch_size] for i in range(0, len(article_ids), batch_size)]

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_articles': len(article_ids),
    'num_batches': len(batches),
    'test_categorize': categorize('War breaks out in Middle East UN Security Council')
}))

args.code:
import json

# Load the 2015 metadata file
file_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:0']
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    metas = json.load(f)

# Let's categorize regions and prepare for processing
regions = {}
for item in metas:
    region = item['region']
    article_id = int(item['article_id'])
    if region not in regions:
        regions[region] = []
    regions[region].append(article_id)

# Sort by region for systematic processing
sorted_regions = {region: sorted(ids) for region, ids in regions.items()}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_articles': len(metas),
    'regions': {r: len(ids) for r, ids in sorted_regions.items()},
    'all_regions': list(sorted_regions.keys())
}))

val_args.code:
import json

# Load the 2015 metadata file
file_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:0']
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    metas = json.load(f)

# Let's categorize regions and prepare for processing
regions = {}
for item in metas:
    region = item['region']
    article_id = int(item['article_id'])
    if region not in regions:
        regions[region] = []
    regions[region].append(article_id)

# Sort by region for systematic processing
sorted_regions = {region: sorted(ids) for region, ids in regions.items()}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_articles': len(metas),
    'regions': {r: len(ids) for r, ids in sorted_regions.items()},
    'all_regions': list(sorted_regions.keys())
}))

args.code:
import json
from collections import Counter, defaultdict

# Load 2015 metadata
meta_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:0']
with open(meta_path, 'r') as f:
    metas = json.load(f)

# Build region lookup
region_lookup = {int(m['article_id']): m['region'] for m in metas}

# Categorization keywords
world_keywords = ['war', 'conflict', 'peace', 'treaty', 'diplomatic', 'ambassador', 'embassy', 'foreign', 'relations', 'border', 'territory', 'province', 'military', 'forces', 'attack', 'violence', 'protest', 'united nations', 'u.n.', 'security council', 'international', 'global', 'world', 'nuclear', 'africa', 'europe', 'asia', 'south america', 'north america', 'middle east', 'israel', 'palestine', 'afghanistan', 'iraq', 'iran', 'syria', 'lebanon', 'pakistan', 'india', 'china', 'russia', 'climate change', 'global warming', 'pandemic', 'epidemic', 'humanitarian', 'aid', 'refugee'] 
sports_keywords = ['football', 'soccer', 'basketball', 'baseball', 'tennis', 'golf', 'olympic', 'championship', 'tournament', 'coach', 'player', 'team', 'season', 'league', 'sport', 'game', 'match', 'medal', 'athlete', 'win', 'lose']
business_keywords = ['stock', 'market', 'IPO', 'company', 'business', 'economy', 'trade', 'deficit', 'profit', 'revenue', 'shares', 'investment', 'bank', 'financial', 'earnings', 'sales', 'fund', 'quarter', 'growth', 'economic', 'job', 'employment']
scitech_keywords = ['technology', 'tech', 'science', 'research', 'study', 'discovery', 'innovation', 'digital', 'software', 'hardware', 'internet', 'computer', 'NASA', 'space', 'DNA', 'genetic', 'medical', 'physics', 'chemistry', 'engineering', 'satellite', 'robot', 'algorithm']

def categorize(title, description):
    """Categorize article based on title and description"""
    if not title and not description:
        return 'Other'
    
    text = f"{title} {description}".lower()
    
    # Score each category
    scores = {
        'World': sum(1 for kw in world_keywords if kw in text),
        'Sports': sum(1 for kw in sports_keywords if kw in text),
        'Business': sum(1 for kw in business_keywords if kw in text),
        'Science/Technology': sum(1 for kw in scitech_keywords if kw in text)
    }
    
    # Find max score
    max_score = max(scores.values())
    if max_score == 0:
        return 'Other'
    
    # Return category with highest score (prioritize in order if tie)
    for category in ['World', 'Sports', 'Business', 'Science/Technology']:
        if scores[category] == max_score:
            return category
    
    return 'Other'

# Test categorization
test_cases = [
    ('Iraq Halts Oil Exports', 'Pipeline attack in southern Iraq'),
    ('Google IPO', 'Google initial public offering'),
    ('NASA launches satellite', 'Space exploration mission'),
    ('Team wins championship', 'Sports victory')
]

test_results = [categorize(title, desc) for title, desc in test_cases]

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_articles_to_process': len(metas),
    'test_categorization': test_results
}))

val_args.code:
import json
from collections import Counter, defaultdict

# Load 2015 metadata
meta_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:0']
with open(meta_path, 'r') as f:
    metas = json.load(f)

# Build region lookup
region_lookup = {int(m['article_id']): m['region'] for m in metas}

# Categorization keywords
world_keywords = ['war', 'conflict', 'peace', 'treaty', 'diplomatic', 'ambassador', 'embassy', 'foreign', 'relations', 'border', 'territory', 'province', 'military', 'forces', 'attack', 'violence', 'protest', 'united nations', 'u.n.', 'security council', 'international', 'global', 'world', 'nuclear', 'africa', 'europe', 'asia', 'south america', 'north america', 'middle east', 'israel', 'palestine', 'afghanistan', 'iraq', 'iran', 'syria', 'lebanon', 'pakistan', 'india', 'china', 'russia', 'climate change', 'global warming', 'pandemic', 'epidemic', 'humanitarian', 'aid', 'refugee'] 
sports_keywords = ['football', 'soccer', 'basketball', 'baseball', 'tennis', 'golf', 'olympic', 'championship', 'tournament', 'coach', 'player', 'team', 'season', 'league', 'sport', 'game', 'match', 'medal', 'athlete', 'win', 'lose']
business_keywords = ['stock', 'market', 'IPO', 'company', 'business', 'economy', 'trade', 'deficit', 'profit', 'revenue', 'shares', 'investment', 'bank', 'financial', 'earnings', 'sales', 'fund', 'quarter', 'growth', 'economic', 'job', 'employment']
scitech_keywords = ['technology', 'tech', 'science', 'research', 'study', 'discovery', 'innovation', 'digital', 'software', 'hardware', 'internet', 'computer', 'NASA', 'space', 'DNA', 'genetic', 'medical', 'physics', 'chemistry', 'engineering', 'satellite', 'robot', 'algorithm']

def categorize(title, description):
    """Categorize article based on title and description"""
    if not title and not description:
        return 'Other'
    
    text = f"{title} {description}".lower()
    
    # Score each category
    scores = {
        'World': sum(1 for kw in world_keywords if kw in text),
        'Sports': sum(1 for kw in sports_keywords if kw in text),
        'Business': sum(1 for kw in business_keywords if kw in text),
        'Science/Technology': sum(1 for kw in scitech_keywords if kw in text)
    }
    
    # Find max score
    max_score = max(scores.values())
    if max_score == 0:
        return 'Other'
    
    # Return category with highest score (prioritize in order if tie)
    for category in ['World', 'Sports', 'Business', 'Science/Technology']:
        if scores[category] == max_score:
            return category
    
    return 'Other'

# Test categorization
test_cases = [
    ('Iraq Halts Oil Exports', 'Pipeline attack in southern Iraq'),
    ('Google IPO', 'Google initial public offering'),
    ('NASA launches satellite', 'Space exploration mission'),
    ('Team wins championship', 'Sports victory')
]

test_results = [categorize(title, desc) for title, desc in test_cases]

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_articles_to_process': len(metas),
    'test_categorization': test_results
}))

import json
from collections import Counter, defaultdict

# Load metadata
meta_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:0']
with open(meta_path, 'r') as f:
    metas = json.load(f)

# Create lookups
region_lookup = {}
for m in metas:
    region_lookup[int(m['article_id'])] = m['region']

world_count = defaultdict(int)
region_totals = defaultdict(int)

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({'status': 'setup_complete', 'total_articles': len(metas)}))

args.code:
import json
from collections import Counter, defaultdict

# Load 2015 metadata
meta_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:0']
with open(meta_path, 'r') as f:
    metas = json.load(f)

# Build lookups
region_by_id = {}
article_ids = []
for m in metas:
    aid = int(m['article_id'])
    article_ids.append(aid)
    region_by_id[aid] = m['region']

# Process in much smaller batches (20 articles at a time)
batch_size = 20
batches = [article_ids[i:i+batch_size] for i in range(0, len(article_ids), batch_size)]

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_articles': len(article_ids),
    'num_batches': len(batches),
    'sample_batch': batches[0][:5]
}))

val_args.code:
import json
from collections import Counter, defaultdict

# Load 2015 metadata
meta_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:0']
with open(meta_path, 'r') as f:
    metas = json.load(f)

# Build lookups
region_by_id = {}
article_ids = []
for m in metas:
    aid = int(m['article_id'])
    article_ids.append(aid)
    region_by_id[aid] = m['region']

# Process in much smaller batches (20 articles at a time)
batch_size = 20
batches = [article_ids[i:i+batch_size] for i in range(0, len(article_ids), batch_size)]

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_articles': len(article_ids),
    'num_batches': len(batches),
    'sample_batch': batches[0][:5]
}))

var_functions.query_db:30.title: Indictments Using DNA on Rise Nationally (AP)
var_functions.query_db:30.description: AP - Authorities once had no choice but to drop rape cases if they weren't able to catch a suspect before the statute of limitations expired. But prosecutors across the country increasingly are buying themselves time, keeping cold cases alive by indicting unidentified rapists using their DNA profiles.

args.code:
import json

# Load metadata
meta_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:0']
with open(meta_path, 'r') as f:
    metas = json.load(f)

# Load articles
articles_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:74']
with open(articles_file, 'r') as f:
    all_articles = json.load(f)

# Create quick lookup
id_to_region = {int(m['article_id']): m['region'] for m in metas}
ids_2015 = set(id_to_region.keys())

# Filter to 2015 articles
articles_2015 = [a for a in all_articles if int(a['article_id']) in ids_2015]

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_2015_articles': len(articles_2015),
    'total_metadata': len(metas),
    'ids_match': len(ids_2015)
}))

val_args.code:
import json

# Load metadata
meta_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:0']
with open(meta_path, 'r') as f:
    metas = json.load(f)

# Load articles
articles_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:74']
with open(articles_file, 'r') as f:
    all_articles = json.load(f)

# Create quick lookup
id_to_region = {int(m['article_id']): m['region'] for m in metas}
ids_2015 = set(id_to_region.keys())

# Filter to 2015 articles
articles_2015 = [a for a in all_articles if int(a['article_id']) in ids_2015]

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_2015_articles': len(articles_2015),
    'total_metadata': len(metas),
    'ids_match': len(ids_2015)
}))

args.code:
import json
from collections import Counter, defaultdict

# Load 2015 metadata
meta_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:0']
with open(meta_path, 'r') as f:
    metas = json.load(f)

# Load articles (10,000 articles)
articles_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:86']
with open(articles_path, 'r') as f:
    all_articles = json.load(f)

# Create lookups
id_to_region = {int(m['article_id']): m['region'] for m in metas}
ids_2015 = set(id_to_region.keys())

# Filter to 2015 articles
articles_2015 = [a for a in all_articles if int(a['article_id']) in ids_2015]

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_articles_loaded': len(all_articles),
    'articles_from_2015': len(articles_2015),
    'total_2015_expected': len(metas)
}))

args.code:
import json
from collections import Counter, defaultdict

# Load 2015 metadata
metadata_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:0']
with open(metadata_path, 'r') as f:
    metas = json.load(f)

# Create lookups
id_to_region = {int(m['article_id']): m['region'] for m in metas}
total_2015 = len(metas)

# Check max article_id to understand range
max_id = max(id_to_region.keys())
min_id = min(id_to_region.keys())

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_2015_articles': total_2015,
    'article_id_range': f"{min_id} to {max_id}",
    'unique_regions': list(set(m['region'] for m in metas))
}))

val_args.code:
import json
from collections import Counter, defaultdict

# Load 2015 metadata
metadata_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:0']
with open(metadata_path, 'r') as f:
    metas = json.load(f)

# Create lookups
id_to_region = {int(m['article_id']): m['region'] for m in metas}
total_2015 = len(metas)

# Check max article_id to understand range
max_id = max(id_to_region.keys())
min_id = min(id_to_region.keys())

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_2015_articles': total_2015,
    'article_id_range': f"{min_id} to {max_id}",
    'unique_regions': list(set(m['region'] for m in metas))
}))

args.code:
import json
from collections import Counter, defaultdict

# Load 2015 metadata
meta_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:0']
with open(meta_path, 'r') as f:
    metas = json.load(f)

# Create mappings
id_to_region = {int(m['article_id']): m['region'] for m in metas}
article_ids = sorted(id_to_region.keys())

# Process in reasonable batches (100 articles)
batch_size = 100
batches = [article_ids[i:i+batch_size] for i in range(0, len(article_ids), batch_size)]

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_articles': len(article_ids),
    'num_batches': len(batches),
    'first_batch': batches[0],
    'last_batch': batches[-1][:5]
}))

val_args.code:
import json
from collections import Counter, defaultdict

# Load 2015 metadata
meta_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:0']
with open(meta_path, 'r') as f:
    metas = json.load(f)

# Create mappings
id_to_region = {int(m['article_id']): m['region'] for m in metas}
article_ids = sorted(id_to_region.keys())

# Process in reasonable batches (100 articles)
batch_size = 100
batches = [article_ids[i:i+batch_size] for i in range(0, len(article_ids), batch_size)]

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_articles': len(article_ids),
    'num_batches': len(batches),
    'first_batch': batches[0],
    'last_batch': batches[-1][:5]
}))

var_functions.query_db:30.title: NASA's Genesis Spacecraft Adjusts Course (AP)
var_functions.query_db:30.description: AP - NASA's Genesis spacecraft successfully adjusted its course this week as it heads back toward Earth with a sample of solar wind particles, the space agency said Wednesday.

var_functions.query_db:30.title: Indictments Using DNA on Rise Nationally (AP)
var_functions.query_db:30.description: AP - Authorities once had no choice but to drop rape cases if they weren't able to catch a suspect before the statute of limitations expired. But prosecutors across the country increasingly are buying themselves time, keeping cold cases alive by indicting unidentified rapists using their DNA profiles.

var_functions.query_db:90.title: A Daily Look at U.S. Iraq Military Deaths (AP)

var_functions.query_db:90.title: Jacobsen Skips Hartford to Play Tradition (AP)

var_functions.query_db:90.title: Alta prison bans pedophile from socials with kids that fuel his fantasies (Canadian Press)
var_functions.query_db:90.description: Canadian Press - WINNIPEG (CP) - A medium-security prison in Alberta has banned a Manitoba pedophile from social functions with the children of other inmates following the man's admission that the events fuel his sexual fantasies.

var_functions.query_db:90.description: ATHENS (Reuters) - Fani Halkia hurtled from virtual  obscurity to become Olympic women's 400 meters hurdles champion  on Wednesday, giving Greece a new sports diva to hail but  sparking a barrage of questions over her rise to prominence.

var_functions.query_db:26.description: Against a background of lackluster retail sales in June and July, analysts and merchants still predict a decent, possibly strong, fall selling season for clothes, accessories and home furnishings.

var_functions.query_db:30.title: NASA's Genesis Spacecraft Adjusts Course (AP)
var_functions.query_db:30.description: AP - NASA's Genesis spacecraft successfully adjusted its course this week as it heads back toward Earth with a sample of solar wind particles, the space agency said Wednesday.

var_functions.query_db:30.title: Indictments Using DNA on Rise Nationally (AP)
var_functions.query_db:30.description: AP - Authorities once had no choice but to drop rape cases if they weren't able to catch a suspect before the statute of limitations expired. But prosecutors across the country increasingly are buying themselves time, keeping cold cases alive by indicting unidentified rapists using their DNA profiles.

var_functions.query_db:30.title: 'Madden,' 'ESPN' Football Score in Different Ways
var_functions.query_db:30.description: PROVIDENCE, R.I. (Reuters) - Was absenteeism a little high  on Tuesday among the guys at the office? EA Sports would like  to think it was because "Madden NFL 2005" came out that day,  and some fans of the football simulation are rabid enough to  take a sick day to play it.

var_functions.query_db:30.title: News: Warmer Weather, Human Disturbances Interact to Change Forests
var_functions.query_db:30.description: While a rapidly changing climate may alter the composition of northern Wisconsin's forests, disturbances such as logging also will play a critical role in how these sylvan ecosystems change over time. (University of Wisconsin-Madison press release)

var_functions.query_db:90.title: A Daily Look at U.S. Iraq Military Deaths (AP)
var_functions.query_db:90.description: AP - As of Wednesday, Aug. 25, 964 U.S. service members have died since the beginning of military operations in Iraq in March 2003, according to the Defense Department. Of those, 722 died as a result of hostile action and 242 died of non-hostile causes.

var_functions.query_db:90.title: Jacobsen Skips Hartford to Play Tradition (AP)
var_functions.query_db:90.description: AP - Last year, Peter Jacobsen was too young to play in the Tradition. This year, he has already won a major on the Champions Tour as he readies for his debut in the tournament he helped bring to his home state.

var_functions.query_db:90.title: Alta prison bans pedophile from socials with kids that fuel his fantasies (Canadian Press)
var_functions.query_db:90.description: Canadian Press - WINNIPEG (CP) - A medium-security prison in Alberta has banned a Manitoba pedophile from social functions with the children of other inmates following the man's admission that the events fuel his sexual fantasies.

var_functions.query_db:90.title: Olympics: Fame But Questions for Greece's New Heroine
var_functions.query_db:90.description: ATHENS (Reuters) - Fani Halkia hurtled from virtual  obscurity to become Olympic women's 400 meters hurdles champion  on Wednesday, giving Greece a new sports diva to hail but  sparking a barrage of questions over her rise to prominence.

var_functions.query_db:90.title: U.S. Expresses Concern About UAL Pensions
var_functions.query_db:90.description: WASHINGTON (Reuters) - The Bush administration expressed  fresh concern on Wednesday about the possibility bankrupt  United Airlines <A HREF="http://www.investor.reuters.com/FullQuote.aspx?ticker=UALAQ.OB target=/stocks/quickinfo/fullquote">UALAQ.OB</A> might scrap its employee pension  plans, which would shift the burden of paying benefits to the  government.

var_functions.query_db:90.title: A Daily Look at U.S. Iraq Military Deaths (AP)
var_functions.query_db:90.description: AP - As of Wednesday, Aug. 25, 964 U.S. service members have died since the beginning of military operations in Iraq in March 2003, according to the Defense Department. Of those, 722 died as a result of hostile action and 242 died of non-hostile causes.

var_functions.query_db:90.title: Jacobsen Skips Hartford to Play Tradition (AP)

var_functions.query_db:90.title: Alta prison bans pedophile from socials with kids that fuel his fantasies (Canadian Press)
var_functions.query_db:90.description: Canadian Press - WINNIPEG (CP) - A medium-security prison in Alberta has banned a Manitoba pedophile from social functions with the children of other inmates following the man's admission that the events fuel his sexual fantasies.

var_functions.query_db:90.title: Olympics: Fame But Questions for Greece's New Heroine
var_functions.query_db:90.description: ATHENS (Reuters) - Fani Halkia hurtled from virtual  obscurity to become Olympic women's 400 meters hurdles champion  on Wednesday, giving Greece a new sports diva to hail but  sparking a barrage of questions over her rise to prominence.

var_functions.query_db:90.title: U.S. Expresses Concern About UAL Pensions

args.code:
import json
from collections import defaultdict

# Load 2015 metadata
metadata_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:0']
with open(metadata_file, 'r') as f:
    metas_2015 = json.load(f)

# Create region mapping
region_by_id = {}
for m in metas_2015:
    region_by_id[int(m['article_id'])] = m['region']

all_ids = sorted(region_by_id.keys())

# Process first batch of 100 articles
first_batch = all_ids[:100]

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_2015': len(metas_2015),
    'first_batch_size': len(first_batch),
    'sample_ids': first_batch[:5],
    'sample_regions': {str(aid): region_by_id[aid] for aid in first_batch[:5]}
}))

val_args.code:
import json
from collections import defaultdict

# Load 2015 metadata
metadata_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:0']
with open(metadata_file, 'r') as f:
    metas_2015 = json.load(f)

# Create region mapping
region_by_id = {}
for m in metas_2015:
    region_by_id[int(m['article_id'])] = m['region']

all_ids = sorted(region_by_id.keys())

# Process first batch of 100 articles
first_batch = all_ids[:100]

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_2015': len(metas_2015),
    'first_batch_size': len(first_batch),
    'sample_ids': first_batch[:5],
    'sample_regions': {str(aid): region_by_id[aid] for aid in first_batch[:5]}
}))

args.code:
import json
from collections import defaultdict

# Load 2015 metadata
meta_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:0']
with open(meta_file, 'r') as f:
    metas = json.load(f)

# Build lookups
region_by_id = {}
all_ids = []
for m in metas:
    aid = int(m['article_id'])
    region_by_id[aid] = m['region']
    all_ids.append(aid)

all_ids.sort()

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total': len(metas),
    'min_id': min(all_ids),
    'max_id': max(all_ids)
}))

val_args.code:
import json
from collections import defaultdict

# Load 2015 metadata
meta_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:0']
with open(meta_file, 'r') as f:
    metas = json.load(f)

# Build lookups
region_by_id = {}
all_ids = []
for m in metas:
    aid = int(m['article_id'])
    region_by_id[aid] = m['region']
    all_ids.append(aid)

all_ids.sort()

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total': len(metas),
    'min_id': min(all_ids),
    'max_id': max(all_ids)
}))

args.code:
import json
from collections import defaultdict

# Load 2015 metadata
meta_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:0']
with open(meta_path, 'r') as f:
    metas = json.load(f)

# Create region lookup
region_by_id = {}
for m in metas:
    region_by_id[int(m['article_id'])] = m['region']

# Group by region initially
world_counts = defaultdict(int)
total_per_region = defaultdict(int)

for m in metas:
    region = m['region']
    total_per_region[region] += 1

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_2015': len(metas),
    'region_totals': dict(total_per_region)

val_args.code:
import json
from collections import defaultdict

# Load 2015 metadata
meta_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:0']
with open(meta_path, 'r') as f:
    metas = json.load(f)

# Create region lookup
region_by_id = {}
for m in metas:
    region_by_id[int(m['article_id'])] = m['region']

# Group by region initially
world_counts = defaultdict(int)
total_per_region = defaultdict(int)

for m in metas:
    region = m['region']
    total_per_region[region] += 1

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_2015': len(metas),
    'region_totals': dict(total_per_region)
}))

var_functions.query_db:106.title: Wall St. Bears Claw Back Into the Black (Reuters)
var_functions.query_db:106.description: Reuters - Short-sellers, Wall Street's dwindling\band of ultra-cynics, are seeing green again.

var_functions.query_db:106.title: Carlyle Looks Toward Commercial Aerospace (Reuters)
var_functions.query_db:106.description: Reuters - Private investment firm Carlyle Group,\which has a reputation for making well-timed and occasionally\controversial plays in the defense industry, has quietly placed\its bets on another part of the market.

var_functions.query_db:106.title: Oil and Economy Cloud Stocks' Outlook (Reuters)
var_functions.query_db:106.description: Reuters - Soaring crude prices plus worries\about the economy and the outlook for earnings are expected to\hang over the stock market next week during the depth of the\summer doldrums.

var_functions.query_db:106.title: Iraq Halts Oil Exports from Main Southern Pipeline (Reuters)
var_functions.query_db:106.description: Reuters - Authorities have halted oil export\flows from the main pipeline in southern Iraq after\intelligence showed a rebel militia could strike\infrastructure, an oil official said on Saturday.

var_functions.query_db:106.title: Oil prices soar to all-time record, posing new menace to US economy (AFP)
var_functions.query_db:106.description: AFP - Tearaway world oil prices, toppling records and straining wallets, present a new economic menace barely three months before the US presidential elections.

var_functions.query_db:110.title: Google IPO Auction Off to Rocky Start
var_functions.query_db:110.description: WASHINGTON/NEW YORK (Reuters) - The auction for Google  Inc.'s highly anticipated initial public offering got off to a  rocky start on Friday after the Web search company sidestepped  a bullet from U.S. securities regulators.

var_functions.query_db:110.title: Dollar Falls Broadly on Record Trade Gap
var_functions.query_db:110.description: NEW YORK (Reuters) - The dollar tumbled broadly on Friday  after data showing a record U.S. trade deficit in June cast  fresh doubts on the economy's recovery and its ability to draw  foreign capital to fund the growing gap.

var_functions.query_db:110.title: Rescuing an Old Saver
var_functions.query_db:110.description: If you think you may need to help your elderly relatives with their finances, don't be shy about having the money talk -- soon.

var_functions.query_db:110.title: Kids Rule for Back-to-School
var_functions.query_db:110.description: The purchasing power of kids is a big part of why the back-to-school season has become such a huge marketing phenomenon.

var_functions.query_db:110.title: In a Down Market, Head Toward Value Funds
var_functions.query_db:110.description: There is little cause for celebration in the stock market these days, but investors in value-focused mutual funds have reason to feel a bit smug -- if only because they've lost less than the folks who stuck with growth.
